name: Next.js Docker with GitHub Container Registry
description: Deploy Next.js application using Docker with GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io)
requirements: GitHub account with container registry access

parameters.name: APP_NAME
parameters.description: Application name (used for Docker image tag)

steps.name: Create project directories
steps.description: Create necessary directories for Docker deployment

steps.name: Pull from GitHub registry
steps.description: Pull latest image from GitHub Container Registry

steps.name: Remove old container
steps.description: Stop and remove old running container if exists
steps.command: docker stop ${APP_NAME} || true && docker rm ${APP_NAME} || true

steps.name: Run Docker container
steps.description: Start new Docker container with environment variables
steps.command: docker run -d --name ${APP_NAME} -p ${PORT}:3000 -e NODE_ENV=${NODE_ENV} -e MONGODB_URI=${MONGODB_URI} -e JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET} --restart unless-stopped ${GITHUB_REGISTRY}/${GITHUB_OWNER}/${APP_NAME}:latest

steps.name: Verify container health
steps.description: Wait for container to be healthy

steps.name: Check application logs
steps.description: Check application logs to verify startup

documentation.title: Next.js Docker Deployment
documentation.description: Complete guide for deploying Next.js applications using Docker

sections.title: Prerequisites
sections.content: Docker must be installed and running on the server. GitHub Container Registry credentials must be configured.

sections.title: Deployment Process
sections.content: This template pulls a pre-built Docker image from GitHub Container Registry and runs it with environment variables.

sections.title: Environment Variables
sections.content: All sensitive data (MONGODB_URI, JWT_SECRET) should be set as secrets in your deployment system.
